approved_control.objective: Perform implementation-level SOTA convergence for the HTTPS proxy contest task: audit the existing implementation, repair protocol and safety issues, add hard tests, fair benchmark evidence, and report revisions.
approved_control.what_counts_as_done: All required outcomes and result paths are directly evidenced; verification commands pass; final report claims are constrained to proven evidence; no required outcome is offset by another outcome; SOTA verified additionally requires the predeclared HTTPS proxy core matrix to pass all hard thresholds against a fair same-model libcurl-library baseline: throughput geomean ratio >= 2.00 using the lower bound of the 95% confidence interval; every scenario throughput ratio >= 1.50 using the lower bound; every scenario p50 latency ratio <= 0.60 using the upper bound; every scenario p95 latency ratio <= 0.70 using the upper bound; at least one flagship scenario reaches throughput ratio >= 3.00 or p95 latency ratio <= 0.50; CPU time per successful request <= 0.80x libcurl-library geometric mean and <= 1.00x in every scenario; peak RSS <= 1.10x in every scenario; both clients have zero protocol, TLS, proxy, and request errors; at least 5 measured repetitions after warmup; coefficient of variation <= 5% or confidence intervals still satisfy every hard threshold; both clients use the same proxy/origin topology, TLS verification, mTLS material, request payload, concurrency, connection reuse, timeout policy, and response validation.

required_steps.transition: Current HTTPS proxy implementation is audited before patches are applied.
HTTPS proxy CONNECT handling follows required protocol semantics and fails closed on invalid responses.
Proxy TLS and proxy mutual TLS are verified end to end and kept distinct from origin TLS.
Sync and async proxy semantics are verified and accurately represented.
A contest-critical HTTPS proxy test matrix is implemented and run.
Benchmark methodology and claims are fair, reproducible, and correctly labeled.
README/report narrative is revised to match verified implementation and benchmark evidence.
SOTA performance status is evaluated against the predeclared numeric hard-threshold matrix.
Pin and review the exact ylong_runtime source used by the ylong_base benchmark build without changing SOTA target semantics.
Instrument or trace runtime readiness/parking around nested HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy I/O and show a causal gap relative to libcurl/tokio observations.
Implement a behavior-preserving runtime readiness/parking candidate and prove it does not change HTTP/TLS/proxy semantics.
Derive an architecture-level upper-bound and candidate-surface review from current S8/S9 evidence before any further runtime implementation attempt.
Only if S9E identifies a still-plausible implementation surface, implement one behavior-preserving candidate with a RED/GREEN or diagnostic test first, then run focused and canonical matrices.
Run final counterexample review only after S9F evidence passes the unchanged fixed SOTA thresholds.

invalidated_evidence: runtime-readiness-candidate canonical matrix as SOTA proof
dequeue fast-path canonical matrix as SOTA proof
block_on-driver focused matrix as SOTA proof
block_on-atomic focused matrix as SOTA proof
any rejected benchmark-only runtime candidate as real-client SOTA evidence
